attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- commit: Stage and commit changes with a conventional commit message
- improve-codebase-architecture: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase, informed by the domain language in CONTEXT.md and the decisions in docs/adr/. Use when the user wants to improve architecture, find refactoring opportunities, consolidate tightly-coupled modules, or make a codebase more testable and AI-navigable.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- triage: Triage issues through a state machine driven by triage roles. Use when user wants to create an issue, triage issues, review incoming bugs or feature requests, prepare issues for an AFK agent, or manage issue workflow.
- prototype: Build a throwaway prototype to flush out a design before committing to it. Routes between two branches — a runnable terminal app for state/business-logic questions, or several radically different UI variations toggleable from one route. Use when the user wants to prototype, sanity-check a data model or state machine, mock up a UI, explore design options, or says "prototype this", "let me play with it", "try a few designs".
- diagnose: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test. Use when user says "diagnose this" / "debug this", reports a bug, says something is broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression.
- to-issues: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices. Use when user wants to convert a plan into issues, create implementation tickets, or break down work into issues.
- write-a-skill: Create new agent skills with proper structure, progressive disclosure, and bundled resources. Use when user wants to create, write, or build a new skill.
- grill-with-docs: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan against their project's language and documented decisions.
- caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler, articles, and pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman.
- tdd: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
- grill-me: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".
- to-prd: Turn the current conversation context into a PRD and publish it to the project issue tracker. Use when user wants to create a PRD from the current context.
- update-features-and-daily-log: Update docs/features.md and docs/daily-log.md from today's merged PRs and commits. Use when the user asks to log today's changes, refresh the feature inventory, or runs /update-features-and-daily-log at end of day.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile my findings:

## AgentCenter Repository Report

### 1. User Table Schema

**File**: `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter/lib/db/schema/auth.ts`

The `users` table carries:
- `id` (primary key)
- `email` (unique, not null)
- `emailVerified` (boolean)
- `name` (optional)
- `image` (optional)
- `locale` (enum: "en" | "zh", default "en")
- `themePreference` (enum: "ivory" | "dark", default "ivory")
- `defaultDeptId` (logical reference to departments, FK omitted to keep auth/org acyclic)
- `createdAt`, `updatedAt` (timestamps with timezone)

Also present: `sessions` and `accounts` tables (better-auth standard tables). No separate profile table exists; the app extends better-auth defaults with `locale`, `themePreference`, and `defaultDeptId`.

### 2. Existing Profile Routes

No "me"/"profile"/"account" user-facing route exists under `app/[locale]/...`. Routes found: `(auth)/sign-in`, `(auth)/sign-up`, `cli/auth`, `extensions`, `publish`, `onboard`. The app doesn't expose a dedicated profile/settings page yet.

### 3. Server Actions vs API Routes

**Both patterns exist**:
- **Server Actions**: `/lib/actions/publish.ts` (e.g., `createDraftExtension`, `submitForReview`, `updateDraftExtension`). All marked with `"use server"`.
- **API Routes**: `/app/api/v1/extensions/route.ts` (GET handler), `/app/api/auth/[...all]/route.ts` (better-auth), `/app/api/v1/auth/device/...` (device flow).

Example server action mutation (lines 1-56 of publish.ts shows `createDraftExtension` structure with validation).

### 4. Validation

**Zod schemas in `/lib/validators/`**: 
- `manifest.ts`: `ExtensionManifestCore` (core field constraints), `ManifestFormSchema` (wizard form), `BundleManifestSchema`, `PermissionsSchema`.
- `filters.ts`: `filtersSchema` (search/filter query validation).

Pattern: Export Zod schemas from dedicated validator files, use `z.infer<typeof Schema>` for type safety. Example from manifest.ts lines 14-31.

### 5. Testing

**Test runner**: Vitest (configured in `vitest.config.ts`)  
**Test locations**:
- Unit tests: `lib/**/*.test.ts`, `cli/**/*.test.ts` (node environment)
- Component tests: `components/**/*.test.tsx` (happy-dom environment via pragma)
- E2E tests: Playwright in `tests/e2e/` (separate from unit tests)

**npm/bun scripts** (package.json lines 13-16):
```
"test": "vitest run"
"test:watch": "vitest"
"test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage"
"test:e2e": "playwright test"
```

**Example component test**: `/components/layout/user-button.test.tsx` (114 lines)—mocks `next-intl`, navigation, and auth client; tests rendering, sign-in link, avatar display, dropdown, sign-out flow, and ARIA attributes.

**Example server-side test**: `/lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` (80+ lines)—mocks Drizzle DB client and auth session; tests `getDraft`, `discardDraft`, `createDraftExtension` with focus on ownership/permission checks. Uses vi.fn() for chained query mocks.

### 6. CI/CD

**GitHub Actions**: `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter/.github/workflows/ci.yml`

Workflow runs on push to main and PRs. Jobs:
```yaml
- Lint (bun run lint)
- Typecheck (bun run typecheck)
- Test (bun run test)  # Vitest
```
No explicit E2E run in main CI; separate `e2e.yml` workflow exists.

content.content:
1	import { boolean, pgTable, text, timestamp } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
2	
3	// Better Auth tables. Drizzle adapter expects these column names; do not rename
4	// without checking the Better Auth schema reference.
5	
6	export const localeEnum = ["en", "zh"] as const;
7	export const themePreferenceEnum = ["ivory", "dark"] as const;
8	
9	export const users = pgTable("users", {
10	  id: text().primaryKey(),
11	  email: text().notNull().unique(),
12	  emailVerified: boolean().notNull().default(false),
13	  name: text(),
14	  image: text(),
15	  locale: text({ enum: localeEnum }).notNull().default("en"),
16	  themePreference: text({ enum: themePreferenceEnum }).notNull().default("ivory"),
17	  // Logical reference to departments.id; FK omitted to keep the auth/org modules
18	  // acyclic. App code is responsible for keeping this in sync.
19	  defaultDeptId: text(),
20	  createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
21	  updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
22	});
23	
24	export const sessions = pgTable("sessions", {
25	  id: text().primaryKey(),
26	  userId: text()
27	    .notNull()
28	    .references(() => users.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
29	  expiresAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull(),
30	  token: text().notNull().unique(),
31	  ipAddress: text(),
32	  userAgent: text(),
33	  createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
34	  updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
35	});
36	
37	export const accounts = pgTable("accounts", {
38	  id: text().primaryKey(),
39	  userId: text()
40	    .notNull()
41	    .references(() => users.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
42	  accountId: text().notNull(),
43	  providerId: text().notNull(),
44	  accessToken: text(),
45	  refreshToken: text(),
46	  idToken: text(),
47	  accessTokenExpiresAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
48	  refreshTokenExpiresAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
49	  scope: text(),
50	  password: text(),
51	  createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
52	  updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
53	});
54	
55	export const verifications = pgTable("verifications", {
56	  id: text().primaryKey(),
57	  identifier: text().notNull(),
58	  value: text().notNull(),
59	  expiresAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull(),
60	  createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
61	  updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
62	});

aiTitle: Implement user profile page with TDD

questions.question: I can't view the design (the URL returned a gzipped blob). Which field set should the profile page edit for v1?

options.label: Name + department (Recommended)
options.description: Editable: display name, default department. Display-only: email, joined-at. Avatar shows initials. No schema change. Locale/theme stay on the topbar switchers (they already persist via better-auth fields).

options.label: Name + dept + bio + avatar URL
options.description: Adds two columns to the users table via a new migration (bio text, image url editable). Slightly larger diff: migration + db:apply step + extra form fields.

options.label: Name only
options.description: Smallest possible v1 — just name. Everything else display-only. Easiest to land, but you'll likely follow up immediately to add dept.

input.content:
# Plan: User profile (“My Workspace”) page

## Context

The marketplace has auth, an avatar in the top-bar dropdown, and a `users` table — but no profile page yet. The design at `…/d0hS3CzPVAe2zbeQJ5jh3Q` calls it **My Workspace**: a hub at `/profile` with a hero strip + a 6-section rail (Installed · Published · Drafts · Saved · Activity · Settings), where Settings is further split into 3 sub-tabs (Profile · Notifications · API tokens).

This PR ships the **shell + the only fully-editable section** (Settings → Profile) so users can land somewhere, edit their basics, and sign out. The five other sections render as "coming soon" empty states that we wire to real data in follow-up PRs — each section's data already exists in the schema (`installs`, `collections.systemKind`, `extensions.publisherUserId`, `ratings`), so they're cleanly carve-able.

User's hard constraints:
1. Avatar dropdown → "Profile" item navigates to the page (dropdown stays — avatar isn't a direct link).
2. Test-driven: write the failing test first for each behavior-bearing step.
3. One commit per coherent step.

## Target design (recap from the bundled HTML, `…/AgentCenter.html` lines 3206–3800)

- **Hero strip** (full width): large initials avatar (no upload v1), serif display name, muted row of `role · dept · joined`, then 4 mini stats (installed / published / total installs / avg rating). `role` is a static i18n label ("Senior Engineer" / "高级工程师") per the design, **not** a per-user field.
- **Left rail** (sticky, 200px): 6 items with icons and counts where applicable. Active item highlights with `accent`-tinted background.
- **Right panel**: section content.
- **Settings → Profile** sub-tab (the editable form): 2-column grid with Name, Email (read-only), Department, Joined (read-only); full-width Bio textarea; full-width Save button.
- **Settings → Notifications**: four toggle rows. **Settings → API tokens**: list + Create button. Both stubbed this PR.

## Scope

**In scope (this PR):**
- Avatar dropdown gets a "Profile" item that links to `/profile`. The existing Sign-out stays.
- New route `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` (RSC), gated by `getSession()`, redirects to `/sign-in` when signed out.
- Hero strip with avatar / name / static role / dept label resolved from `DEPARTMENTS` / joined date from `users.createdAt`. Stats slots present but **rendered with placeholder zeros** in this PR (real counts in follow-ups).
- Section rail with `?section=` URL routing (sharable, refresh-safe). Default `installed`.
- Settings → Profile sub-tab: editable Name + Department + Bio; read-only Email + Joined. Save calls a new `updateProfile` server action.
- Settings → Notifications + API tokens render the design's empty state with a "Coming soon" label.
- Installed / Published / Drafts / Saved / Activity sections render the same coming-soon empty state.
- en + zh i18n keys for everything above.

**Schema change:** add `bio text` column to `users`. (No `role` column — the design uses a static label.)

**Out of scope (explicit follow-ups, one per section):**
- Wiring Installed / Published / Drafts / Saved / Activity to real data.
- Hero stat counts.
- Notifications + API tokens functionality.
- Avatar image upload, email/password change. None of these appear in the design's Profile sub-tab.

## Step plan (one commit each, TDD where there's logic to test)

### 1. i18n keys for the `profile.*` namespace (en + zh)
Pure-data commit; no test. Add a `profile` namespace to `lib/i18n/messages/en.json` and `…/zh.json` with the keys we'll consume across all steps (hero, rail labels, settings tab labels, settings form labels, coming-soon copy). Mirror what's in the design HTML at lines 137–203 (en) and 441–507 (zh).
- **Commit:** `chore(i18n): add profile namespace strings`

### 2. Add "Profile" link to avatar dropdown
- **RED:** extend `components/layout/user-button.test.tsx` with a test that opens the dropdown and asserts a link named `profile` exists with `href="/profile"`, ordered before the Sign out button.
- **GREEN:** insert a `<Link href="/profile">` row in `components/layout/user-button.tsx` (between the name/email header and the Sign-out button), styled to match the existing sign-out row. Use `t("profile")` from the existing `auth.userMenu` translator (new key in step 1).
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): link avatar dropdown to profile page`

### 3. DB schema: add `bio` to `users`
- Update `lib/db/schema/auth.ts` to add `bio: text()` (nullable).
- Run `bun run db:generate` (or whatever the existing script is — verify via `package.json`) to create the next migration file under `drizzle/`.
- No test added; relying on `bun run typecheck` + existing migration apply path.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): add bio column to users`

### 4. `updateProfile` server action + Zod schema
- **RED:** new `lib/actions/user.test.ts` — mirror the mock pattern in `lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` (Drizzle client + auth session mocked with `vi.fn`). Cover:
  - rejects when no session (`{ ok: false, error: "unauthenticated" }`)
  - rejects when Zod validation fails (returns `error: "invalid_input"` + detail)
  - happy path: writes `name`, `defaultDeptId`, `bio` to the current user row only
- **GREEN:**
  - New `lib/validators/profile.ts` exporting `ProfileFormSchema` (name 1–80 chars, bio max 280, defaultDeptId optional string).
  - Extend `lib/actions/user.ts` (currently only has `updateDepartment`) with `updateProfile(values)`. Reuse `getSession()` from `lib/auth/session.ts`. Keep `updateDepartment` as-is.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): updateProfile server action and validators`

### 5. Profile route + Hero strip
- **RED:** new `components/profile/profile-hero.test.tsx` (happy-dom pragma). Cover: renders initials from `name`, falls back to first email letter when name is null; renders department label resolved from `DEPARTMENTS` data; renders formatted joined month/year string. Mirror the mocking style from `user-button.test.tsx`.
- **GREEN:**
  - New `components/profile/profile-hero.tsx` (client OK, but can stay server-only — props in, JSX out, no state).
  - New `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` as an RSC: `getSession()`, redirect to `/sign-in` if absent; load the user row by id (`db.select … from users where id = …`); render `<ProfileHero/>` + a `<SectionRail/>` placeholder + the active section's body. Add `generateMetadata` returning the `profile.title` translation (matches the pattern in `app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx:11`).
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): profile route shell with hero strip`

### 6. Section rail with URL-driven routing
- **RED:** new `components/profile/section-rail.test.tsx`. Cover: renders 6 items, the one matching the passed `activeKey` prop has `aria-current="page"`, every item is an `<a>` whose href encodes `?section=<key>`.
- **GREEN:**
  - New `components/profile/section-rail.tsx` — uses `Link` from `@/lib/i18n/navigation`; receives `activeKey` and emits href with `?section=...` preserving locale.
  - In `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx`, parse `searchParams.section` with a small `parseSection()` helper (whitelist of 6 keys, fallback to `installed`) and pass into the rail + a switch that renders the matching body.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): section rail with URL-driven routing`

### 7. Settings → Profile sub-tab (the editable form)
This is the only section with real behavior in this PR.
- **RED:** new `components/profile/profile-settings-form.test.tsx`. Cover:
  - renders Name, Department, Bio inputs prefilled from the `user` prop
  - renders Email + Joined as disabled
  - on submit, calls `updateProfile` with the form values (mocked) and shows a success state when it returns `{ ok: true }`
  - on validation error from the action, surfaces an inline error
- **GREEN:**
  - New `components/profile/profile-settings-form.tsx` — `"use client"`, controlled inputs, `useTransition` + `useState` for pending/success/error states (same idiom as `app/[locale]/onboard/page.tsx`).
  - Wire the form as the body of `section=settings` with `settingsTab=profile` default. Sub-tab routing via a second query param `?tab=profile|notifications|tokens` parsed in the same RSC.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): editable Settings → Profile form`

### 8. Coming-soon stubs for the remaining sections + sub-tabs
- One file `components/profile/coming-soon.tsx` exporting a small empty-state component, parameterized by section title + supporting copy.
- Wire 5 main sections (Installed, Published, Drafts, Saved, Activity) and 2 sub-tabs (Notifications, API tokens) to render it.
- No new test; coverage on `profile-settings-form.test.tsx` already exercises sub-tab routing once tabs exist.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): stub Installed/Published/Drafts/Saved/Activity + Notifications/Tokens`

## Critical files

| Concern | File |
|---|---|
| i18n catalogs | `lib/i18n/messages/en.json`, `lib/i18n/messages/zh.json` |
| Avatar dropdown | `components/layout/user-button.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |
| Schema | `lib/db/schema/auth.ts`, generated `drizzle/0003_*.sql` |
| Validator | `lib/validators/profile.ts` (new) |
| Server action | `lib/actions/user.ts` (extend, keep `updateDepartment`) (+ new `lib/actions/user.test.ts`) |
| Route | `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` (new RSC) |
| Hero | `components/profile/profile-hero.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |
| Rail | `components/profile/section-rail.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |
| Settings form | `components/profile/profile-settings-form.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |
| Stubs | `components/profile/coming-soon.tsx` |

## Reused utilities (don't re-implement)

- `getSession()` — `lib/auth/session.ts:7` (RSC auth gate)
- `authClient.useSession()` — `lib/auth/client.ts:8` (already used by `UserButton`)
- `Link`, `useRouter` from `@/lib/i18n/navigation` (locale-aware; see `top-bar.tsx`)
- `useTranslations` / `getTranslations` from `next-intl` (client/server split as in `app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx:12`)
- `DEPARTMENTS` and the recursive flatten helper pattern from `app/[locale]/onboard/page.tsx:11` for the dept Select
- `db` and `users` from `lib/db/client`, `lib/db/schema/auth` for the action update
- Test mocking idiom from `components/layout/user-button.test.tsx` (happy-dom + mocking `next-intl`, `@/lib/i18n/navigation`, `@/lib/auth/client`) and `lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` (Drizzle + session vi.fn mocks)

## Verification

After each commit:
- `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test` — green.

After step 3 (migration):
- `bun run db:apply` (or the project's existing script — confirm via `package.json`) to apply the new migration to the local Neon DB.

End-to-end (after step 8, manually in dev):
- Sign in → click avatar → dropdown shows **Profile** above **Sign out** → click Profile.
- `/profile` renders the hero + rail; default section = Installed = coming-soon.
- Navigate via rail: URL updates to `?section=settings`; Settings section renders with tabs.
- On Settings → Profile: edit Name and Bio → Save → success state visible; refresh page → values persist.
- Sign out from the dropdown still works.
- Switch locale to `zh` → all new copy is translated.

## Open question flagged for the user (not blocking; my default below)

The design doesn't show a dropdown — clicking the avatar opens the workspace directly. User requirement (1) says keep the dropdown with a Profile item. **Default I'm going with:** keep the existing dropdown structure exactly, add a "Profile" item between the email header and the Sign out button.

- **Hero strip** (full width): large initials avatar (no upload v1), serif display name, muted row of `role · dept · joined`, then 4 mini stats (installed / published / total installs / avg rating). `role` is a static i18n label ("Senior Engineer" / "高级工程师") per the design, **not** a per-user field.
- **Left rail** (sticky, 200px): 6 items with icons and counts where applicable. Active item highlights with `accent`-tinted background.
- **Right panel**: section content.
- **Settings → Profile** sub-tab (the editable form): 2-column grid with Name, Email (read-only), Department, Joined (read-only); full-width Bio textarea; full-width Save button.
- **Settings → Notifications**: four toggle rows. **Settings → API tokens**: list + Create button. Both stubbed this PR.

## Scope

**In scope (this PR):**
- Avatar dropdown gets a "Profile" item that links to `/profile`. The existing Sign-out stays.
- New route `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` (RSC), gated by `getSession()`, redirects to `/sign-in` when signed out.
- Hero strip with avatar / name / static role / dept label resolved from `DEPARTMENTS` / joined date from `users.createdAt`. Stats slots present but **rendered with placeholder zeros** in this PR (real counts in follow-ups).
- Section rail with `?section=` URL routing (sharable, refresh-safe). Default `installed`.
- Settings → Profile sub-tab: editable Name + Department + Bio; read-only Email + Joined. Save calls a new `updateProfile` server action.
- Settings → Notifications + API tokens render the design's empty state with a "Coming soon" label.
- Installed / Published / Drafts / Saved / Activity sections render the same coming-soon empty state.
- en + zh i18n keys for everything above.

**Schema change:** add `bio text` column to `users`. (No `role` column — the design uses a static label.)

**Out of scope (explicit follow-ups, one per section):**
- Wiring Installed / Published / Drafts / Saved / Activity to real data.
- Hero stat counts.
- Notifications + API tokens functionality.
- Avatar image upload, email/password change. None of these appear in the design's Profile sub-tab.

## Step plan (one commit each, TDD where there's logic to test)

### 1. i18n keys for the `profile.*` namespace (en + zh)
Pure-data commit; no test. Add a `profile` namespace to `lib/i18n/messages/en.json` and `…/zh.json` with the keys we'll consume across all steps (hero, rail labels, settings tab labels, settings form labels, coming-soon copy). Mirror what's in the design HTML at lines 137–203 (en) and 441–507 (zh).
- **Commit:** `chore(i18n): add profile namespace strings`

### 2. Add "Profile" link to avatar dropdown
- **RED:** extend `components/layout/user-button.test.tsx` with a test that opens the dropdown and asserts a link named `profile` exists with `href="/profile"`, ordered before the Sign out button.
- **GREEN:** insert a `<Link href="/profile">` row in `components/layout/user-button.tsx` (between the name/email header and the Sign-out button), styled to match the existing sign-out row. Use `t("profile")` from the existing `auth.userMenu` translator (new key in step 1).
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): link avatar dropdown to profile page`

### 3. DB schema: add `bio` to `users`
- Update `lib/db/schema/auth.ts` to add `bio: text()` (nullable).
- Run `bun run db:generate` (or whatever the existing script is — verify via `package.json`) to create the next migration file under `drizzle/`.
- No test added; relying on `bun run typecheck` + existing migration apply path.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): add bio column to users`

### 4. `updateProfile` server action + Zod schema
- **RED:** new `lib/actions/user.test.ts` — mirror the mock pattern in `lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` (Drizzle client + auth session mocked with `vi.fn`). Cover:
  - rejects when no session (`{ ok: false, error: "unauthenticated" }`)
  - rejects when Zod validation fails (returns `error: "invalid_input"` + detail)
  - happy path: writes `name`, `defaultDeptId`, `bio` to the current user row only
- **GREEN:**
  - New `lib/validators/profile.ts` exporting `ProfileFormSchema` (name 1–80 chars, bio max 280, defaultDeptId optional string).
  - Extend `lib/actions/user.ts` (currently only has `updateDepartment`) with `updateProfile(values)`. Reuse `getSession()` from `lib/auth/session.ts`. Keep `updateDepartment` as-is.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): updateProfile server action and validators`

### 5. Profile route + Hero strip
- **RED:** new `components/profile/profile-hero.test.tsx` (happy-dom pragma). Cover: renders initials from `name`, falls back to first email letter when name is null; renders department label resolved from `DEPARTMENTS` data; renders formatted joined month/year string. Mirror the mocking style from `user-button.test.tsx`.
- **GREEN:**
  - New `components/profile/profile-hero.tsx` (client OK, but can stay server-only — props in, JSX out, no state).
  - New `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` as an RSC: `getSession()`, redirect to `/sign-in` if absent; load the user row by id (`db.select … from users where id = …`); render `<ProfileHero/>` + a `<SectionRail/>` placeholder + the active section's body. Add `generateMetadata` returning the `profile.title` translation (matches the pattern in `app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx:11`).
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): profile route shell with hero strip`

### 6. Section rail with URL-driven routing
- **RED:** new `components/profile/section-rail.test.tsx`. Cover: renders 6 items, the one matching the passed `activeKey` prop has `aria-current="page"`, every item is an `<a>` whose href encodes `?section=<key>`.
- **GREEN:**
  - New `components/profile/section-rail.tsx` — uses `Link` from `@/lib/i18n/navigation`; receives `activeKey` and emits href with `?section=...` preserving locale.
  - In `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx`, parse `searchParams.section` with a small `parseSection()` helper (whitelist of 6 keys, fallback to `installed`) and pass into the rail + a switch that renders the matching body.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): section rail with URL-driven routing`

### 7. Settings → Profile sub-tab (the editable form)
This is the only section with real behavior in this PR.
- **RED:** new `components/profile/profile-settings-form.test.tsx`. Cover:
  - renders Name, Department, Bio inputs prefilled from the `user` prop
  - renders Email + Joined as disabled
  - on submit, calls `updateProfile` with the form values (mocked) and shows a success state when it returns `{ ok: true }`
  - on validation error from the action, surfaces an inline error
- **GREEN:**
  - New `components/profile/profile-settings-form.tsx` — `"use client"`, controlled inputs, `useTransition` + `useState` for pending/success/error states (same idiom as `app/[locale]/onboard/page.tsx`).
  - Wire the form as the body of `section=settings` with `settingsTab=profile` default. Sub-tab routing via a second query param `?tab=profile|notifications|tokens` parsed in the same RSC.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): editable Settings → Profile form`

### 8. Coming-soon stubs for the remaining sections + sub-tabs
- One file `components/profile/coming-soon.tsx` exporting a small empty-state component, parameterized by section title + supporting copy.
- Wire 5 main sections (Installed, Published, Drafts, Saved, Activity) and 2 sub-tabs (Notifications, API tokens) to render it.
- No new test; coverage on `profile-settings-form.test.tsx` already exercises sub-tab routing once tabs exist.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): stub Installed/Published/Drafts/Saved/Activity + Notifications/Tokens`

## Critical files

| Concern | File |
|---|---|
| i18n catalogs | `lib/i18n/messages/en.json`, `lib/i18n/messages/zh.json` |
| Avatar dropdown | `components/layout/user-button.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |
| Schema | `lib/db/schema/auth.ts`, generated `drizzle/0003_*.sql` |
| Validator | `lib/validators/profile.ts` (new) |
| Server action | `lib/actions/user.ts` (extend, keep `updateDepartment`) (+ new `lib/actions/user.test.ts`) |
| Route | `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` (new RSC) |
| Hero | `components/profile/profile-hero.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |
| Rail | `components/profile/section-rail.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |
| Settings form | `components/profile/profile-settings-form.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |
| Stubs | `components/profile/coming-soon.tsx` |

## Reused utilities (don't re-implement)

- `getSession()` — `lib/auth/session.ts:7` (RSC auth gate)
- `authClient.useSession()` — `lib/auth/client.ts:8` (already used by `UserButton`)
- `Link`, `useRouter` from `@/lib/i18n/navigation` (locale-aware; see `top-bar.tsx`)
- `useTranslations` / `getTranslations` from `next-intl` (client/server split as in `app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx:12`)
- `DEPARTMENTS` and the recursive flatten helper pattern from `app/[locale]/onboard/page.tsx:11` for the dept Select
- `db` and `users` from `lib/db/client`, `lib/db/schema/auth` for the action update
- Test mocking idiom from `components/layout/user-button.test.tsx` (happy-dom + mocking `next-intl`, `@/lib/i18n/navigation`, `@/lib/auth/client`) and `lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` (Drizzle + session vi.fn mocks)

## Verification

After each commit:
- `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test` — green.

After step 3 (migration):
- `bun run db:apply` (or the project's existing script — confirm via `package.json`) to apply the new migration to the local Neon DB.

End-to-end (after step 8, manually in dev):
- Sign in → click avatar → dropdown shows **Profile** above **Sign out** → click Profile.
- `/profile` renders the hero + rail; default section = Installed = coming-soon.
- Navigate via rail: URL updates to `?section=settings`; Settings section renders with tabs.
- On Settings → Profile: edit Name and Bio → Save → success state visible; refresh page → values persist.
- Sign out from the dropdown still works.
- Switch locale to `zh` → all new copy is translated.

## Open question flagged for the user (not blocking; my default below)

The design doesn't show a dropdown — clicking the avatar opens the workspace directly. User requirement (1) says keep the dropdown with a Profile item. **Default I'm going with:** keep the existing dropdown structure exactly, add a "Profile" item between the email header and the Sign out button.

  - rejects when Zod validation fails (returns `error: "invalid_input"` + detail)
  - happy path: writes `name`, `defaultDeptId`, `bio` to the current user row only
- **GREEN:**
  - New `lib/validators/profile.ts` exporting `ProfileFormSchema` (name 1–80 chars, bio max 280, defaultDeptId optional string).
  - Extend `lib/actions/user.ts` (currently only has `updateDepartment`) with `updateProfile(values)`. Reuse `getSession()` from `lib/auth/session.ts`. Keep `updateDepartment` as-is.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): updateProfile server action and validators`

### 5. Profile route + Hero strip
- **RED:** new `components/profile/profile-hero.test.tsx` (happy-dom pragma). Cover: renders initials from `name`, falls back to first email letter when name is null; renders department label resolved from `DEPARTMENTS` data; renders formatted joined month/year string. Mirror the mocking style from `user-button.test.tsx`.
- **GREEN:**
  - New `components/profile/profile-hero.tsx` (client OK, but can stay server-only — props in, JSX out, no state).
  - New `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` as an RSC: `getSession()`, redirect to `/sign-in` if absent; load the user row by id (`db.select … from users where id = …`); render `<ProfileHero/>` + a `<SectionRail/>` placeholder + the active section's body. Add `generateMetadata` returning the `profile.title` translation (matches the pattern in `app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx:11`).
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): profile route shell with hero strip`

### 6. Section rail with URL-driven routing
- **RED:** new `components/profile/section-rail.test.tsx`. Cover: renders 6 items, the one matching the passed `activeKey` prop has `aria-current="page"`, every item is an `<a>` whose href encodes `?section=<key>`.
- **GREEN:**
  - New `components/profile/section-rail.tsx` — uses `Link` from `@/lib/i18n/navigation`; receives `activeKey` and emits href with `?section=...` preserving locale.
  - In `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx`, parse `searchParams.section` with a small `parseSection()` helper (whitelist of 6 keys, fallback to `installed`) and pass into the rail + a switch that renders the matching body.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): section rail with URL-driven routing`

### 7. Settings → Profile sub-tab (the editable form)
This is the only section with real behavior in this PR.
- **RED:** new `components/profile/profile-settings-form.test.tsx`. Cover:
  - renders Name, Department, Bio inputs prefilled from the `user` prop
  - renders Email + Joined as disabled
  - on submit, calls `updateProfile` with the form values (mocked) and shows a success state when it returns `{ ok: true }`
  - on validation error from the action, surfaces an inline error
- **GREEN:**
  - New `components/profile/profile-settings-form.tsx` — `"use client"`, controlled inputs, `useTransition` + `useState` for pending/success/error states (same idiom as `app/[locale]/onboard/page.tsx`).
  - Wire the form as the body of `section=settings` with `settingsTab=profile` default. Sub-tab routing via a second query param `?tab=profile|notifications|tokens` parsed in the same RSC.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): editable Settings → Profile form`

### 8. Coming-soon stubs for the remaining sections + sub-tabs
- One file `components/profile/coming-soon.tsx` exporting a small empty-state component, parameterized by section title + supporting copy.
- Wire 5 main sections (Installed, Published, Drafts, Saved, Activity) and 2 sub-tabs (Notifications, API tokens) to render it.
- No new test; coverage on `profile-settings-form.test.tsx` already exercises sub-tab routing once tabs exist.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): stub Installed/Published/Drafts/Saved/Activity + Notifications/Tokens`

|---|---|
| i18n catalogs | `lib/i18n/messages/en.json`, `lib/i18n/messages/zh.json` |
| Avatar dropdown | `components/layout/user-button.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |

| Rail | `components/profile/section-rail.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |
| Settings form | `components/profile/profile-settings-form.tsx` (+ `.test.tsx`) |
| Stubs | `components/profile/coming-soon.tsx` |

## Reused utilities (don't re-implement)

- `getSession()` — `lib/auth/session.ts:7` (RSC auth gate)
- `authClient.useSession()` — `lib/auth/client.ts:8` (already used by `UserButton`)

toolUseResult.plan:
# Plan: User profile (“My Workspace”) page

## Context

The marketplace has auth, an avatar in the top-bar dropdown, and a `users` table — but no profile page yet. The design at `…/d0hS3CzPVAe2zbeQJ5jh3Q` calls it **My Workspace**: a hub at `/profile` with a hero strip + a 6-section rail (Installed · Published · Drafts · Saved · Activity · Settings), where Settings is further split into 3 sub-tabs (Profile · Notifications · API tokens).

This PR ships the **shell + the only fully-editable section** (Settings → Profile) so users can land somewhere, edit their basics, and sign out. The five other sections render as "coming soon" empty states that we wire to real data in follow-up PRs — each section's data already exists in the schema (`installs`, `collections.systemKind`, `extensions.publisherUserId`, `ratings`), so they're cleanly carve-able.

User's hard constraints:
1. Avatar dropdown → "Profile" item navigates to the page (dropdown stays — avatar isn't a direct link).
2. Test-driven: write the failing test first for each behavior-bearing step.
3. One commit per coherent step.

## Target design (recap from the bundled HTML, `…/AgentCenter.html` lines 3206–3800)

- **Hero strip** (full width): large initials avatar (no upload v1), serif display name, muted row of `role · dept · joined`, then 4 mini stats (installed / published / total installs / avg rating). `role` is a static i18n label ("Senior Engineer" / "高级工程师") per the design, **not** a per-user field.
- **Left rail** (sticky, 200px): 6 items with icons and counts where applicable. Active item highlights with `accent`-tinted background.
- **Right panel**: section content.
- **Settings → Profile** sub-tab (the editable form): 2-column grid with Name, Email (read-only), Department, Joined (read-only); full-width Bio textarea; full-width Save button.
- **Settings → Notifications**: four toggle rows. **Settings → API tokens**: list + Create button. Both stubbed this PR.

## Scope

**In scope (this PR):**
- Avatar dropdown gets a "Profile" item that links to `/profile`. The existing Sign-out stays.
- New route `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` (RSC), gated by `getSession()`, redirects to `/sign-in` when signed out.
- Hero strip with avatar / name / static role / dept label resolved from `DEPARTMENTS` / joined date from `users.createdAt`. Stats slots present but **rendered with placeholder zeros** in this PR (real counts in follow-ups).
- Section rail with `?section=` URL routing (sharable, refresh-safe). Default `installed`.
- Settings → Profile sub-tab: editable Name + Department + Bio; read-only Email + Joined. Save calls a new `updateProfile` server action.
- Settings → Notifications + API tokens render the design's empty state with a "Coming soon" label.
- Installed / Published / Drafts / Saved / Activity sections render the same coming-soon empty state.
- en + zh i18n keys for everything above.

**Schema change:** add `bio text` column to `users`. (No `role` column — the design uses a static label.)

**Out of scope (explicit follow-ups, one per section):**
- Wiring Installed / Published / Drafts / Saved / Activity to real data.
- Hero stat counts.
- Notifications + API tokens functionality.
- Avatar image upload, email/password change. None of these appear in the design's Profile sub-tab.

## Step plan (one commit each, TDD where there's logic to test)

### 1. i18n keys for the `profile.*` namespace (en + zh)
Pure-data commit; no test. Add a `profile` namespace to `lib/i18n/messages/en.json` and `…/zh.json` with the keys we'll consume across all steps (hero, rail labels, settings tab labels, settings form labels, coming-soon copy). Mirror what's in the design HTML at lines 137–203 (en) and 441–507 (zh).
- **Commit:** `chore(i18n): add profile namespace strings`

### 2. Add "Profile" link to avatar dropdown
- **RED:** extend `components/layout/user-button.test.tsx` with a test that opens the dropdown and asserts a link named `profile` exists with `href="/profile"`, ordered before the Sign out button.
- **GREEN:** insert a `<Link href="/profile">` row in `components/layout/user-button.tsx` (between the name/email header and the Sign-out button), styled to match the existing sign-out row. Use `t("profile")` from the existing `auth.userMenu` translator (new key in step 1).
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): link avatar dropdown to profile page`

### 3. DB schema: add `bio` to `users`
- Update `lib/db/schema/auth.ts` to add `bio: text()` (nullable).
- Run `bun run db:generate` (or whatever the existing script is — verify via `package.json`) to create the next migration file under `drizzle/`.
- No test added; relying on `bun run typecheck` + existing migration apply path.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): add bio column to users`

### 4. `updateProfile` server action + Zod schema
- **RED:** new `lib/actions/user.test.ts` — mirror the mock pattern in `lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts` (Drizzle client + auth session mocked with `vi.fn`). Cover:
  - rejects when no session (`{ ok: false, error: "unauthenticated" }`)
  - rejects when Zod validation fails (returns `error: "invalid_input"` + detail)
  - happy path: writes `name`, `defaultDeptId`, `bio` to the current user row only

  - New `lib/validators/profile.ts` exporting `ProfileFormSchema` (name 1–80 chars, bio max 280, defaultDeptId optional string).
  - Extend `lib/actions/user.ts` (currently only has `updateDepartment`) with `updateProfile(values)`. Reuse `getSession()` from `lib/auth/session.ts`. Keep `updateDepartment` as-is.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): updateProfile server action and validators`

### 5. Profile route + Hero strip

Cover: renders initials from `name`, falls back to first email letter when name is null; renders department label resolved from `DEPARTMENTS` data; renders formatted joined month/year string. ### 7. Settings → Profile sub-tab (the editable form)
This is the only section with real behavior in this PR.
- **RED:** new `components/profile/profile-settings-form.test.tsx`. Cover:

  - Wire the form as the body of `section=settings` with `settingsTab=profile` default. Sub-tab routing via a second query param `?tab=profile|notifications|tokens` parsed in the same RSC.
- **Commit:** `feat(profile): editable Settings → Profile form`

### 8. Coming-soon stubs for the remaining sections + sub-tabs
- One file `components/profile/coming-soon.tsx` exporting a small empty-state component, parameterized by section title + supporting copy.
- Wire 5 main sections (Installed, Published, Drafts, Saved, Activity) and 2 sub-tabs (Notifications, API tokens) to render it.
- No new test; coverage on `profile-settings-form.test.tsx` already exercises sub-tab routing once tabs exist.

content: Auto mode lets Claude handle permission prompts automatically — Claude checks each tool call for risky actions and prompt injection before executing. Actions Claude identifies as safe are executed, while actions Claude identifies as risky are blocked and Claude may try a different approach. Ideal for long-running tasks. Sessions are slightly more expensive. Claude can make mistakes that allow harmful commands to run, it's recommended to only use in isolated environments. Shift+Tab to change mode.

1	import { z } from "zod";
2	
3	// Slug shape, exported so client-side form validators don't redefine it.
4	export const SLUG_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/;
5	export const SEMVER_PATTERN = /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/;
6	
7	// "summary" is the redesigned wizard's one-line description; it lands in
8	// the existing `tagline` column. Capped at 80 chars to match the design.
9	export const SUMMARY_MAX = 80;
10	
11	// Single source of truth for what makes an extension manifest valid.
12	// Mirrors `docs/manifest-spec.md`. Bundle and form schemas below derive
13	// from this core; nothing should redefine these constraints elsewhere.
14	export const ExtensionManifestCore = z.object({
15	  slug: z
16	    .string()
17	    .min(3)
18	    .max(64)
19	    .regex(SLUG_PATTERN, "Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens only"),
20	  name: z.string().min(2).max(80),
21	  nameZh: z.string().max(80).optional(),
22	  version: z.string().regex(SEMVER_PATTERN, "Must be semver (e.g. 1.0.0)"),
23	  category: z.enum(["skills", "mcp", "slash", "plugins"]),
24	  scope: z.enum(["personal", "org", "enterprise"]),
25	  funcCat: z.enum(["workTask", "business", "tools"]),
26	  subCat: z.string().min(1).max(60),
27	  l2: z.string().max(60).optional(),
28	  description: z.string().min(1).max(280),
29	  descriptionZh: z.string().max(280).optional(),
30	  tagline: z.string().max(120).optional(),
31	});
32	
33	// Permissions toggled in the wizard — surfaced on the detail page so users
34	// know what the extension intends to do before installing.
35	export const PermissionsSchema = z
36	  .object({
37	    network: z.boolean().optional(),
38	    files: z.boolean().optional(),
39	    runtime: z.boolean().optional(),
40	    data: z.boolean().optional(),

content.text:
All 8 steps shipped on `feat/profile-page`. Summary:

**What landed**
- Avatar dropdown → new **Profile** item linking to `/profile` (above Sign out)
- `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` — RSC, redirects to `/sign-in` when signed out, loads the user row
- Hero strip (avatar initials, name, static role · resolved dept · joined month/year)
- Section rail at `?section=installed|published|drafts|saved|activity|settings` (default `installed`)
- Settings sub-tabs at `?tab=profile|notifications|tokens` (default `profile`)
- Editable **Settings → Profile** form (name + dept + bio; email + joined read-only) → `updateProfile` server action + Zod `ProfileFormSchema`
- `users.bio` column + migration `drizzle/0003_tricky_earthquake.sql` (applied to local DB)
- All five non-Settings sections + the two stub Settings sub-tabs render a shared **ComingSoon** empty state
- en + zh i18n catalogs

**TDD record**: every behavior-bearing step had a RED test before the implementation — user-button (profile link), updateProfile action (6 cases), ProfileHero (4 cases), SectionRail (3 cases), ProfileSettingsForm (4 cases). Final suite: **317 tests, 37 files, all green**. Lint + typecheck clean.

**Smoke test**: unauthenticated `GET /en/profile` on your running dev server returned the page chunk + the expected `<title>My workspace</title>` and the `/sign-in` redirect — the auth gate and i18n hook are wired up. I couldn't drive a real authenticated session from here, so please log in and confirm: dropdown → Profile → hero renders → switch sections via the rail → Settings → Profile → edit name/bio → Save → refresh persists.

**Next slices** (deliberately out of scope of this PR): wiring real data for Installed / Published / Drafts / Saved / Activity, and real Notifications + API tokens — each one is a clean follow-up because the schema (`installs`, `collections.systemKind`, `extensions.publisherUserId`, `ratings`) is already in place.

Branch is ready for `gh pr create` when you want to open it.

gh pr create --title "feat(profile): user profile page (My Workspace)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Adds `/profile` ("My workspace") behind the avatar dropdown: hero strip + 6-section rail + Settings → Profile editable form (name, dept, bio; email + joined are read-only).
- Section rail is URL-driven (`?section=`); Settings sub-tabs use `?tab=`. Five non-Settings sections + the two non-Profile settings sub-tabs render a shared **Coming soon** empty state to leave clean follow-up slices.
- New schema column `users.bio` (migration `0003_tricky_earthquake.sql`), `updateProfile` server action with `ProfileFormSchema` (Zod), and en + zh i18n strings.

Built TDD: every behavior-bearing change has a failing test landed before the implementation (UserButton, updateProfile action, ProfileHero, SectionRail, ProfileSettingsForm — **+23 new tests, suite at 317/317 green**). Each commit is one coherent step (see `git log`).

Scope intentionally tight: Installed / Published / Drafts / Saved / Activity sections and Notifications / API-tokens sub-tabs are stubs in this PR. The underlying schema (`installs`, `collections.systemKind`, `extensions.publisherUserId`, `ratings`) is already in place, so each follow-up slice is small and independent.

## Test plan

- [ ] Sign in → click avatar → dropdown shows **Profile** above **Sign out**, with `/profile` link
- [ ] Click Profile → `/profile` renders hero (initials, name, role · dept · joined) + section rail; default section = Installed = Coming soon

message.content: when I click the tabs in profile page, they all shows: This section is in the works — we'll surface it here once it ships. why?

content.text: That's by design — but let me confirm the wiring is what I described, because if the **Settings → Profile** sub-tab is also showing the placeholder, that's a bug.

Wiring is correct: the **Settings → Profile** sub-tab shows the editable form, the other 5 left-rail items and the Notifications / API-tokens sub-tabs intentionally show "Coming soon". I called this out in the PR summary as the scope cut for this slice. Five of six rail items + 2 of 3 Settings sub-tabs are placeholders.

Each is a clean, independent follow-up because the data is already in the schema:

| Rail item | Data source already in place |
|---|---|
| Installed | `installs` table, `lib/installs/record.ts` already records via web + CLI |
| Published | `extensions.publisherUserId` (the `/publish` dashboard already queries it via `getMyExtensions`) |
| Drafts | same `extensions` table filtered by `visibility = 'draft'` |
| Saved | `collections` with `systemKind = 'saved'` |
| Activity | `installs` + `extensionVersions.publishedAt` + `ratings` (no separate activity log; aggregate from those) |

Want me to land them in one follow-up PR or one per section? I'd recommend one PR per section — they're small, easy to review, and let CodeRabbit catch any data-shape issues per slice. Top of the list would be **Published** since the query already exists in `lib/actions/publish.ts`.

message.content: I think we can land them all in one follow-up PR, what do you think?

lastPrompt: I think we can land them all in one follow-up PR, what do you think?

content.text:
Yes, one PR works — these sections are mostly the same shape (list of extension cards bound to a different query), so the diff converges on a shared row component + 5 thin section components + 5 small queries. The risk of "big diff" is muted because there's basically one pattern repeated.

If we go this route I'd structure it as ~7 commits on a `feat/profile-sections` branch:

1. Shared `ExtensionRowCard` component (TDD — one render test covering name, tagline, install button slot)
2. **Published** — already have `getMyExtensions` in `lib/actions/publish.ts`, reuse it
3. **Installed** — new `lib/queries/profile.ts` with `getInstalledForUser()`, filtered by `installs.uninstalledAt IS NULL`
4. **Drafts** — query `extensions` where `publisherUserId = me AND visibility = 'draft'`; reuse the resume-edit link from publish dashboard
5. **Saved** — query through `collections` where `systemKind = 'saved' AND ownerUserId = me` + `collection_items`
6. **Activity** — aggregate `installs` + `extensionVersions.publishedAt` + `ratings` for the current user, sort desc, top 20; activity row component
7. **Hero stats** — wire the 4 counters (installed / published / total-installs-on-my-extensions / avg rating) from the queries we now have

Activity is the only one that requires real thought (it's a union of three event sources). Want me to start, or do you want to grill the activity design first?

content.content:
coderabbitai[bot] @ components/profile/profile-hero.tsx:20
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Guard against edge cases in initials generation.**

The `initialsFor` function has potential runtime issues:
1. If `name` is an empty string `""`, it will pass the truthy check but `name.split(" ")` produces `[""]`, causing `w[0]` on Line 14 to be undefined.
2. Consecutive spaces in `name` (e.g., `"Alice  Bob"`) create empty strings in the split array, also leading to undefined access.
3. If `email` is empty (though typed as non-nullable `string`), Line 19 would fail.



<details>
<summary>🛡️ Proposed fix with defensive checks</summary>

```diff
 function initialsFor(name: string | null, email: string): string {
-  if (name) {
+  if (name && name.trim()) {
     return name
       .split(" ")
+      .filter((w) => w.length > 0)
       .map((w) => w[0])
       .join("")
       .slice(0, 2)
       .toUpperCase();
   }
-  return email[0].toUpperCase();
+  return email[0]?.toUpperCase() || "?";
 }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@components/profile/profile-hero.tsx` around lines 10 - 20, The initialsFor
function currently trusts name and email contents and can crash on empty strings
or multiple spaces; update initialsFor to trim the name, split on whitespace,
filter out empty segments, and map safely (guarding against undefined
characters) to produce up to two initials (uppercase); if the resulting initials
string is empty, fall back to the first character of email (trimmed) if present,
otherwise return a safe default like "?" to avoid runtime errors; reference the
initialsFor function to implement these defensive checks.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:puma -->

<!-- 4e71b3a2 -->

coderabbitai[bot] @ lib/validators/profile.ts:14
_🛠️ Refactor suggestion_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Remove redundant `.optional()` before `.default()`.**

In Zod 4, `.default(value)` already makes the input optional and provides the default when undefined. Chaining `.optional().default(value)` is redundant—the `.optional()` has no effect.


<details>
<summary>♻️ Simplify to use only .default()</summary>

```diff
 export const ProfileFormSchema = z.object({
   name: z.string().min(PROFILE_NAME_MIN).max(PROFILE_NAME_MAX),
-  defaultDeptId: z.string().max(120).optional().default(""),
-  bio: z.string().max(PROFILE_BIO_MAX).optional().default(""),
+  defaultDeptId: z.string().max(120).default(""),
+  bio: z.string().max(PROFILE_BIO_MAX).default(""),
 });
```
</details>

As per coding guidelines, Zod 4 documentation: "`.default()` now short-circuits parsing when input is `undefined` and directly returns the default."

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
export const ProfileFormSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(PROFILE_NAME_MIN).max(PROFILE_NAME_MAX),
  defaultDeptId: z.string().max(120).default(""),
  bio: z.string().max(PROFILE_BIO_MAX).default(""),
});
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@lib/validators/profile.ts` around lines 13 - 14, The Zod schema properties
defaultDeptId and bio are using redundant .optional() before .default(); remove
the .optional() call so each field uses only .default(...) (e.g., change
defaultDeptId: z.string().max(120).optional().default("") →
z.string().max(120).default("") and bio:
z.string().max(PROFILE_BIO_MAX).optional().default("") →
z.string().max(PROFILE_BIO_MAX).default("")) to rely on Zod 4's default behavior
that makes the input optional and returns the default when undefined.
```

</details>

lastPrompt: let's check the PR #30 first, what do you think?

content.text:
PR #30 is in good shape: **CI green** (Lint · Typecheck · Test ✓), **MERGEABLE** state CLEAN, no Vercel deploy issues. CodeRabbit posted **2 actionable + 6 nitpicks**. Quick triage:

**Take (real bugs / clean wins):**
- 🟠 `lib/validators/profile.ts:14` — `.optional().default("")` is redundant in Zod 4; drop `.optional()`.
- 🟡 `components/profile/profile-hero.tsx:10–20` — `initialsFor("")` crashes (`"".split(" ")[0][0]` → undefined) and double-spaces produce empty segments. Real edge cases worth guarding.
- ✅ Pin those with the suggested edge-case tests in `profile-hero.test.tsx`.
- ✅ `user-button.tsx:91` — `<User>` icon needs `aria-hidden` (the sibling `<LogOut>` doesn't have it either; I'll fix both to match the rest of the codebase's pattern).
- ✅ Extract `PROFILE_DEPT_ID_MAX = 120` in `lib/validators/profile.ts`.

**Skip + reply:**
- `section-rail.tsx:61` `aria-hidden` → `aria-hidden={true}`: in React, JSX shorthand is identical to `={true}`. Pure style preference, no behavior change.
- `settings-tab-link.tsx` template literal vs `+` concat: pure style. The existing `top-bar.tsx` and others use `+` concat — I'd prefer consistency over a localized switch.

**Flag as follow-up, not this PR:**
- `profile-settings-form.tsx:95–101` — the dept `<select>` uses `"  ".repeat(indent) + "└ "` for hierarchy. CodeRabbit is right on a11y, but the **same exact code** is in `app/[locale]/onboard/page.tsx:73–78` (I copied the pattern). Fixing only here would create inconsistency. Better to fix both in a small a11y PR after this merges, or wait until we adopt a shadcn combobox.

I'd land all the take-on-board items in one commit (`chore: address CodeRabbit feedback on profile PR`) and reply to the skip/follow-up ones on the PR. Want me to proceed?

input.command:
gh pr comment 30 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Thanks @coderabbitai. Pushed `afd0562` addressing the actionable items and the high-signal nits:

- ✅ `initialsFor` guards empty / whitespace-only names and filters consecutive spaces. Pinned with 3 new edge-case tests in `profile-hero.test.tsx` (empty name → falls back to email initial, `"Alice  Bob  Carter"` → `AB`, single-letter name → renders one initial).
- ✅ Dropped redundant `.optional()` before `.default("")` in `ProfileFormSchema` (Zod 4 short-circuits on undefined).
- ✅ Extracted `PROFILE_DEPT_ID_MAX = 120` alongside the other named limits.
- ✅ Marked the dropdown `User` and `LogOut` icons `aria-hidden` (visible label already announced).

**Skipped, with reasons:**

- **`section-rail.tsx:61` `aria-hidden` → `aria-hidden={true}`** — these are equivalent in React (JSX shorthand parses to `={true}`). The "React 19 best practice" claim isn't accurate AFAICT; behavior is identical. The repo uses the shorthand throughout (see `top-bar.tsx`, the existing avatar in `user-button.tsx`, etc.) so switching only this file would be inconsistent.
- **`settings-tab-link.tsx` template literal vs `+` concat** — pure style. Other client components in the repo (`top-bar.tsx`, the sidebar tree) use `+` concat for conditional class strings; flipping just this one creates noise without changing behavior.

**Follow-up, not this PR:**

- **Department `<select>` accessibility** (`profile-settings-form.tsx:95–101`) — fair point on `"  ".repeat() + "└ "` being unfriendly to screen readers. But the *exact same code* lives in `app/[locale]/onboard/page.tsx:73–78` (this PR copied that pattern). Fixing only here would diverge two department pickers. I'd rather do a small a11y PR that fixes both — or replaces both with a shared component / shadcn combobox — once this lands.

CI is green (320 tests). Ready for another look.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

input.prompt:
Explore /Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter for the following and report concrete code excerpts with file paths + line numbers. Under 600 words.

1. **`getMyExtensions` in `lib/actions/publish.ts`**: full signature, what columns it returns, what row shape callers consume. Show how `app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx` reads it.

2. **`installs` table** in `lib/db/schema/activity.ts`: full column list. Is there an `uninstalledAt` column? How does `lib/installs/record.ts` (or wherever) record installs and uninstalls?

3. **`collections` and `collection_items`** in `lib/db/schema/collection.ts`: full column list. What does `systemKind = 'saved'` mean — is there one saved collection per user, auto-created, or per extension? Show any helper / action that toggles "saved" status.

4. **`ratings` table** in `lib/db/schema/activity.ts`: columns (especially aggregation columns).

5. **`extensionVersions`** in `lib/db/schema/extension.ts`: relevant columns for "I published a new version" event timestamps.

6. **Existing "extension card" components**: is there a presentational card already in `components/extension/` (or similar) for listing an extension on a grid/list? If yes, what props does it take? Show the file path. I want to know whether to reuse it or build a tighter row-shape component for the profile sections.

7. **`Locale` typing and how component files import it**: confirm `type Locale = "en" | "zh"` from `@/types`.

8. **Test conventions for queries that hit the DB**: are there existing patterns for testing `lib/queries/*` (mocking Drizzle vs hitting a test DB)? Show one example test file path + the mocking style (mirror of `lib/actions/publish-ownership.test.ts`?).

Report each numbered item with: file path:line, a short excerpt or column list, and one sentence on what the caller currently does.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

---

## Findings Report

**1. `getMyExtensions` in `lib/actions/publish.ts` (line 259–305)**

**Signature:**
```typescript
export async function getMyExtensions(userId: string)
```

**Returned columns** (from the `.select()` object):
- `id`, `slug`, `name`, `category`, `visibility`, `createdAt` (extension fields)
- `latestVersionId`, `latestVersion`, `latestStatus`, `latestBundleFileId` (version fields)
- `latestScanReport` (from files table)
- `latestScanReason` (computed via `extractScanReason()`)

**Row shape callers consume:** Each row is keyed by `extensions.id` (line 297–301), collapsed to one per extension, with computed `latestScanReason` appended. Returns `Array<{id, slug, name, category, visibility, createdAt, latestVersionId, latestVersion, latestStatus, latestBundleFileId, latestScanReport, latestScanReason}>`.

**How `app/[locale]/publish/page.tsx` reads it (line 21):** Calls `getMyExtensions(session.user.id)`, iterates the array, and extracts fields like `ext.latestStatus` (line 70), `ext.latestBundleFileId` (line 70), `ext.visibility`, `ext.name`, `ext.slug`, and `ext.latestScanReason` (line 127) to render the publish dashboard.

---

**2. `installs` table in `lib/db/schema/activity.ts` (line 16–32)**

**Full column list:**
- `id: text().primaryKey()`
- `userId: text().notNull().references(users.id)` 
- `extensionId: text().notNull().references(extensions.id)`
- `version: text().notNull()`
- `source: installSourceEnum()` (values: "cli" or "web")
- `installedAt: timestamp().notNull().defaultNow()`
- **`uninstalledAt: timestamp()`** — Yes, this column exists (line 29).

**Recording uninstalls:** No `recordUninstall` function found in the codebase yet. The `uninstalledAt` column is defined but not populated. `lib/installs/record.ts` only implements `recordInstall()` which inserts an install record and increments the extension's `downloadsCount`.

---

**3. `collections` and `collection_items` in `lib/db/schema/collection.ts` (line 14–38)**

**`collections` columns:**
- `id: text().primaryKey()`
- `ownerUserId: text().notNull().references(users.id)`
- `name: text().notNull()`
- `nameZh: text()`
- `systemKind: systemKindEnum()` — enum("installed" | "saved"), nullable for user-created groups

**`collection_items` columns:**
- `collectionId: text().notNull().references(collections.id)`
- `extensionId: text().notNull().references(extensions.id)`
- `addedAt: timestamp().notNull().defaultNow()`
- Composite PK on (collectionId, extensionId)

**What `systemKind = 'saved'` means:** Per line 8 comment and `lib/db/queries/collections.ts` (line 8–28), there is **one auto-created "saved" system collection per user**. The `getOrCreateSystemCollection(userId, "saved")` function (line 21–27) checks if one exists; if not, creates it with `name: "Saved"` and `systemKind: "saved"`. Users can toggle "saved" status via `lib/actions/collections.ts::saveExtension()` (line 15–28) which upserts the extension into the saved collection.

---

**4. `ratings` table in `lib/db/schema/activity.ts` (line 34–49)**

**Columns:**
- `id: text().primaryKey()`
- `userId: text().notNull().references(users.id)`
- `extensionId: text().notNull().references(extensions.id)`
- `stars: integer().notNull()` — the rating value
- `review: text()` — optional review text
- `createdAt: timestamp().notNull().defaultNow()`
- Unique constraint on (userId, extensionId) — one rating per user per extension

**Aggregation columns:** None on the `ratings` table itself. The `extensions` table has denormalized counters `ratingsCount: integer().default(0)` and `starsAvg: numeric(precision: 2, scale: 1).default("0.0")` (line 115–116) updated by jobs.

---

**5. `extensionVersions` in `lib/db/schema/extension.ts` (line 136–159)**

**Relevant columns for "I published a new version" timestamps:**
- `publishedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true })` — line 155
- `createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow()` — line 156

The `publishedAt` is the "published" event timestamp; `createdAt` is insertion timestamp. Line 68 in `lib/installs/record.ts` uses `desc(extensionVersions.publishedAt)` to find the latest published version.

---

**6. Existing "extension card" component**

**File:** `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter/components/extension/ext-card.tsx`

**Props:**
```typescript
interface ExtCardProps {
  ext: ExtensionListItem;
  locale?: Locale;
}
```

**What it takes:** An `ExtensionListItem` (returned from `lib/db/queries/extensions.ts::listExtensions()`, line 63–65) with fields: `id`, `slug`, `category`, `badge`, `scope`, `funcCat`, `subCat`, `l2`, `deptId`, `iconEmoji`, `iconColor`, `name`, `nameZh`, `description`, `descriptionZh`, `downloadsCount`, `starsAvg`, `tagIds`.

**What it renders:** A full feature card (line 48–133) with icon, name, badge, department pill, description, tags, star/download counts, and action buttons (`SaveButton`, `InstallButton`). This is a complete UI card, not a lightweight row component—reuse for grid/list display is appropriate; building a tighter row-shape component is separate work.

---

**7. `Locale` typing**

**File:** `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter/types/index.ts` (line 1)
```typescript
export type Locale = "en" | "zh";
```

**Import:** Component files import it as `import type { Locale } from "@/types"` (e.g., `ext-card.tsx` line 9).

---

**8. Test conventions for DB queries**

**Pattern:** `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter/lib/installs/record.test.ts` (lines 1–179)

**Mocking style:** Uses Vitest `vi.mock()` to replace the `@/lib/db/client` module (line 8–14) with mocked `select`, `insert`, `update`, `transaction` functions. Each function is a `vi.fn()` that the test wires up with mock implementations. The test also mocks dependent queries (`getOrCreateSystemCollection`, line 18–20).

**No query test file exists** in `lib/db/queries/` yet (verified: no `*.test.ts` in `lib/db/queries/`). Use the `record.test.ts` style (mock the `db` client, chain selects/transactions, assert on mock call counts and payloads) when adding tests for new query functions.

**5. `extensionVersions` in `lib/db/schema/extension.ts` (line 136–159)**

**Relevant columns for "I published a new version" timestamps:**
- `publishedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true })` — line 155
- `createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow()` — line 156

The `publishedAt` is the "published" event timestamp; `createdAt` is insertion timestamp. Line 68 in `lib/installs/record.ts` uses `desc(extensionVersions.publishedAt)` to find the latest published version.

---

**6. Existing "extension card" component**

**File:** `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter/components/extension/ext-card.tsx`

**Props:**
```typescript
interface ExtCardProps {
  ext: ExtensionListItem;
  locale?: Locale;
}
```

**What it takes:** An `ExtensionListItem` (returned from `lib/db/queries/extensions.ts::listExtensions()`, line 63–65) with fields: `id`, `slug`, `category`, `badge`, `scope`, `funcCat`, `subCat`, `l2`, `deptId`, `iconEmoji`, `iconColor`, `name`, `nameZh`, `description`, `descriptionZh`, `downloadsCount`, `starsAvg`, `tagIds`.

**What it renders:** A full feature card (line 48–133) with icon, name, badge, department pill, description, tags, star/download counts, and action buttons (`SaveButton`, `InstallButton`). This is a complete UI card, not a lightweight row component—reuse for grid/list display is appropriate; building a tighter row-shape component is separate work.

---

**7. `Locale` typing**

**File:** `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter/types/index.ts` (line 1)
```typescript
export type Locale = "en" | "zh";
```

**Import:** Component files import it as `import type { Locale } from "@/types"` (e.g., `ext-card.tsx` line 9).

---

**8. Test conventions for DB queries**

**Pattern:** `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter/lib/installs/record.test.ts` (lines 1–179)

**Mocking style:** Uses Vitest `vi.mock()` to replace the `@/lib/db/client` module (line 8–14) with mocked `select`, `insert`, `update`, `transaction` functions. Each function is a `vi.fn()` that the test wires up with mock implementations. The test also mocks dependent queries (`getOrCreateSystemCollection`, line 18–20).

**No query test file exists** in `lib/db/queries/` yet (verified: no `*.test.ts` in `lib/db/queries/`). Use the `record.test.ts` style (mock the `db` client, chain selects/transactions, assert on mock call counts and payloads) when adding tests for new query functions.

export const extensions = pgTable(
  "extensions",
  {
    id: text().primaryKey(),
    slug: text().notNull().unique(),
    category: extensionCategoryEnum().notNull(),
    badge: extensionBadgeEnum(),
    scope: extensionScopeEnum().notNull(),
    // funcCat/subCat are nullable — the redesigned publish wizard does not
    // collect them; admin curation can backfill or system defaults apply.
    funcCat: funcCatEnum(),
    subCat: text(),
    l2: text(),
    publisherUserId: text().references(() => users.id, {
      onDelete: "set null",
    }),
    ownerOrgId: text()
      .notNull()
      .references(() => organizations.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    deptId: text().references(() => departments.id, { onDelete: "set null" }),
    iconEmoji: text(),
    iconColor: text(),
    visibility: visibilityEnum().notNull().default("draft"),
    // i18n columns
    name: text().notNull(),
    nameZh: text(),
    tagline: text(),
    taglineZh: text(),
    description: text(),
    descriptionZh: text(),
    // Long-form markdown body for the detail page. Not duplicated to ZH yet —
    // most extensions will have an English README, with translations layered
    // on later if needed.
    readmeMd: text(),
    // metadata for detail page sidebar
    homepageUrl: text(),
    repoUrl: text(),
    licenseSpdx: text(),
    compatibilityJson: jsonb(),
    // Permissions captured during publish (network/files/runtime/data, etc.).
    // Surfaced on the detail page so users see what the extension wants.
    permissions: jsonb().notNull().default({}),
    // denormalized counters (updated by jobs)
    downloadsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
    starsAvg: numeric({ precision: 2, scale: 1 }).notNull().default("0.0"),
    ratingsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
    publishedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
    createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
    updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat(profile): wire all 5 sections + hero stats to real data" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Follow-up to #30. Replaces the "Coming soon" placeholders for **Installed · Published · Drafts · Saved · Activity** with real data, and wires the four **hero stats** (installed count, published count, total installs of my extensions, weighted avg rating). No schema changes — all data already exists from earlier phases (`installs`, `extensions.publisherUserId`, `collections.systemKind`, `ratings`).

New file `lib/db/queries/profile.ts` carries the six queries. Each section has its own client component + RED-then-GREEN test. The Activity feed merges three sources (installs, my published versions, my ratings) client-side from three indexed selects rather than a single UNION ALL — easier to read, parallelizes, and the limit (20 per source) keeps total rows trivial.

One commit per section + one for hero stats — easy to review in isolation:

- \`462b215\` Published
- \`4a5efec\` Installed
- \`c893173\` Drafts
- \`661034d\` Saved
- \`e128bd2\` Activity
- \`1c061fe\` Hero stats

**Suite: 332 tests, 42 files, all green.** Lint + typecheck clean.

## Test plan

- [ ] Sign in, open \`/profile\` → hero shows 4 stats (zeros are fine on a fresh account); section rail still works
- [ ] **Installed**: install an extension via \`/extensions/<slug>\`, return to \`/profile?section=installed\`, row appears with the installed version. Confirm \`uninstalledAt IS NULL\` rows are the only ones shown (no recordUninstall path exists yet, so empty in practice)
- [ ] **Published**: as a publisher, the published listings appear with their latest \`status='ready'\` version + install counts. Detail-page link works.
- [ ] **Drafts**: any in-progress draft shows here with a Continue button → goes to \`/publish/<extId>/edit\`. Matches the publish-dashboard's resume entry.
- [ ] **Saved**: bookmark an extension via the save action, see it here ordered by most-recently-saved.
- [ ] **Activity**: install/publish/rate events from the current user surface, top 20, most-recent first.
- [ ] Hero stats reflect the same data: installedCount == Installed section count, publishedCount == Published count, totalInstalls is sum of \`downloadsCount\` across my published extensions, avgRating is ratings-weighted (or "—" with zero ratings).
- [ ] Switch to \`/zh/profile\` — all new empty states + activity verbs are translated.
- [ ] \`bun run validate\` — green.

## Open follow-ups (out of scope)

- **Uninstall path**: \`installs.uninstalledAt\` exists but no \`recordUninstall\` action exists yet — the Installed list is the union of all install events for the user filtered by \`uninstalledAt IS NULL\`. Once an Uninstall button lands, no profile-page changes needed.
- **Department-select a11y** (carried from #30): the \`onboard\` and \`profile-settings-form\` dept pickers still use \`"  ".repeat() + "└ "\`. A small a11y PR replaces both.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
PR #31 is CI-green (332 tests, MERGEABLE/CLEAN). CodeRabbit posted **3 actionable**, no nitpicks. My triage:

**Take #1 — `profile-hero.tsx` blank-name `<h1>`**
`displayName = name ?? email` lets `""` or whitespace render an empty heading. I already guarded `initialsFor` against this same shape in PR #30; the heading needs the same treatment. Pin with a regression test.

**Take #3 — Activity "published" query missing `visibility = "published"` guard**
A `ready` version on a `draft` extension would surface as a "published" activity event even though the extension isn't publicly visible. Real bug. Add the guard.

**Modify #2 — `section-drafts.tsx` raw status text** 
CodeRabbit's specific suggestion is wrong (`"pending_upload"` doesn't exist in the enum — actual values are `pending | scanning | ready | rejected`), but the *underlying* concern is right: showing raw `"scanning"` / `"rejected"` is ugly and untranslated. I'll translate only the actionable ones (`scanning`, `rejected`) and hide `pending`/`ready` (a `ready` draft means scanner passed and the user just hasn't clicked Publish yet — no useful status to surface). Will add i18n keys to match.

All three in one commit, push, and reply on the PR. Going.
